response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level background, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise in industrial automation with SDL/IEC 62443 practices. His only professional C++ exposure was a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; his systems work since has been in DLang and Node.js/TypeScript. He shows no MQTT, message-broker-at-scale in C++, industrial protocol, or industrial-cybersecurity experience, making him a poor match despite general seniority.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software experience including low-latency, high-throughput systems (async I/O, fibers, caching at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source authorship
Event-driven architecture and distributed-platform experience (GISCollective, Kubernetes deployments)
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months, a decade ago); primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial-automation protocol experience listed
Career trajectory is web/product-focused, diverging from embedded/industrial C++ systems work

key_factors.factor: Proven low-latency and event-driven systems skills
